concerns.text: User Story 1 and FR-001/FR-002 define a pipeline for 'zero-shot LLM inference' and 'ground-truth alignment' but omit the mandatory baseline comparison required by Constitution Principle VII and the Idea's 'Performance evaluation' step. The spec must explicitly include running static analyzers (Bandit/cppcheck) on the same dataset and recording their metrics for comparison.

User Story 3 and FR-005/FR-006 specify correlation and regression analysis but omit the 'McNemar's test' explicitly required in the Idea's 'Performance evaluation' section to compare LLM predictions against the baseline static analyzer. This is a critical statistical test for the research question.

The Idea's 'Feature extraction' section explicitly lists 'embedding-based similarity to known vulnerable patterns (using a small pre-trained code encoder)' as a predictor. The spec (US-2, FR-003, FR-004) only lists structural (AST, complexity) and semantic (taint API) metrics, dropping the embedding-based feature entirely.

The Idea's 'Performance evaluation' section requires computing 'ROC-AUC' for each model and category. The spec (US-1, FR-005) only mandates precision, recall, and F1-scores, omitting ROC-AUC which is a standard metric for binary classification performance in this context.
